title: abies balsamea subsp. lasiocarpa
intro: Abies balsamea subsp. lasiocarpa, also known as Balsam Fir or Rocky Mountain Balsam Fir, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of compounds like borneol and bornyl acetate. The health benefits of Abies balsamea subsp. lasiocarpa include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as anti-arthritic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is often prepared for herbal use through steam distillation to produce essential oils, and can also be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves.
